text: Just discussing. There is a merge conflict probably because i changed code from laptop as well as this bot. Now what does that mean and what shall I do?

A merge conflict happens when two different sources (you on your laptop, and me via this bot) edit the **same lines of the same file** at the same time. Git can't automatically decide which version to keep, so it marks the conflict and asks you to resolve it manually.

## What happened

```
Your laptop branch:   A → B → C (your changes)
                              ↕ conflict here
Bot's branch:         A → B → D (my changes)
```

Both modified the same lines in `main.py`, so when git tried to m
I want to keep the bot changes. Can you help me with the commands and how I should run it?
Task completed: I want to keep the bot changes. Can you help me with the commands and how I should run it?
When i click on the PR link, it doesn't let me merge as there is a merge conflict message on that page. Refer to attached image
Task completed: When i click on the PR link, it doesn't let me merge as there is a merge conflict message on that page. Refer to attached image
Why did HF not build after i merged this pr?

Here's why HF didn't build after you merged:

## Root Cause

The GitHub Actions workflow (`sync_to_hf.yml`) only triggers on pushes to the **`main` branch**:

```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
```

Looking at the `main` branch, its latest commit is still `6f6ed13 Rebrand to RxPrescribe` — the recent fixes from your feature branch **never landed on main**.

## What Likely Happened

The PR had a merge conflict (as shown in the previous conversation). GitHub blocked the merge because of that

1. In patient account, after clicking AI button, there is overlap between the word Done and the word Check
2. Also in patient account, the button save address needs to be clicked twice to be able to save the address
2. All expandable buttons across the app have the blinking issue
